goal: Make visible portal UI Russian-first while deferring full multi-language localization architecture to a separate decision.
global_constraints: Do not translate machine identifiers, JSON keys, tool ids, database status codes, CSS classes or URLs.
Do not introduce gettext catalogs or language switching in this slice.
Do not change business behavior while translating UI copy.
Keep technical abbreviations such as API, AD, FTS, JSON, MCP, PII and CITO where they are used as terms.
Update documentation, .desc.json and PROJECT_STRUCTURE.yaml after structural changes.
cross_cutting_acceptance: Main navigation and page headings use Russian UI copy.
AI chat and AI hub use Russian UI copy.
Memory review and search index review use Russian UI copy.
Settings Center pages use Russian UI copy.
Django model choice labels used by portal UI are Russian.
Tool and setting descriptions shown in UI are Russian.
Verification commands pass or residual risk is documented.
verification_commands: .venv/bin/python manage.py test apps.core.tests apps.ai.tests apps.memory.tests apps.settings_center.tests apps.workorders.tests apps.waiting_list.tests apps.inventory.tests
E2E_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8000 E2E_USERNAME=chief_manager E2E_PASSWORD=... npm run test:e2e -- --project=chromium
visible text scan for key pages
implementation_results: Portal templates and browser JavaScript use Russian UI copy.
Django labels, forms, setting descriptors, tool descriptions and task type descriptions use Russian UI copy.
Default AI tool/task contracts and runtime data/contracts copies were updated.
Technical identifiers, JSON keys, tool ids and product names remain unchanged.
deferred_decisions: Whether to use Django gettext as the localization backbone.
How to translate JavaScript strings once language switching is introduced.
Where user or organization language preference will be stored.
How runtime contracts and module-registered descriptors will be localized.
Whether CI should fail on visible English strings.
